title: Maw (Dark Jedi)
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Conflicting|[[The Dark Forces Saga|''The Dark Forces Saga, Part 4: Of the Crudest Matter'']]|[[Building a Better Jedi: Justin Chin on Dark Forces II|''Building a Better Jedi'']]}} {{Youmay|the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]]|[[Maw (disambiguation)|other uses of the word "maw"]]}} {{Character |type=Dark Jedi |image=[[File:Maw-JKDFII.jpg]] |name=Maw |homeworld= |birth=Before [[65 BBY/Legends|65 BBY]]{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050526a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 4: Of the Crudest Matter}}''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' places the events of ''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'', which features Maw's death, in [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]]. ''[[The Dark Forces Saga]], Part 4'' describes Maw as being over seventy years old, therefore simple math shows that he was born sometime before [[65 BBY/Legends|65 BBY]]. |death=[[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' places the events of ''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'', which features Maw's death, in [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]]. [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]]''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'' |species=[[Boltrunian/Legends|Boltrunian]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |skin= |cyber=[[Repulsorlift/Legends|Repulsorlift]] lower torso |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] *[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] *[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] *[[Seven Dark Jedi]] |masters=[[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] {{C|informal [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] master}} |apprentices= }} {{Quote|To the one called Maw: a bitter individual that loathes all and holds loyalty to few. A strong and formidable foe. For every cord of muscle, there is hatred. It is this hate that keeps his aging body strong.|[[Qu Rahn/Legends|Qu Rahn]] as a [[Force ghost/Legends|Force ghost]], to [[Kyle Katarn]]|audio=QuRahn-Maw.flac|}} '''Maw''' was a [[Boltrunian/Legends|Boltrunian]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jedi Shadow|Jedi]] who fell to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] and joined [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec's]] [[Seven Dark Jedi|cadre]] of [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]], assisting him in his search for the fabled [[Valley of the Jedi]], which Jerec hoped to use to help rebuild the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], in an effort to learn about the location of the Valley, Jerec attempted to [[Mission to Dorlo|capture]] the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Qu Rahn/Legends|Qu Rahn]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dorlo]]. Maw and fellow Dark Jedi [[Sariss]] and [[Yun]] were assigned to capture Rahn on the surface of the planet, but failed their mission. Although Rahn escaped from the planet aboard a [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]], the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] was captured in the [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] of Jerec's personal [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]], the [[Vengeance (Vengeance-class)|''Vengeance'']]. Aboard the ''Vengeance'', Jerec [[Drain Knowledge|ripped]] the information he needed from Rahn's mind, but immediately afterward, the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] stole Yun's [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and attacked Jerec's [[darksider]]s. Before Rahn was [[Death/Legends|killed]] by Jerec, Maw's [[Limb/Legends|legs]] were [[Sai tok/Legends|severed]] from his body by the Jedi Master. Maw survived and replaced his legs with a [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] carriage, but eventually mastered the art of [[Telekinesis/Legends|Force flight]] and appropriated the [[Trispzest]] form of [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]]. Due to his injury, he also came to be known as '''the Half-Man'''. Eventually, Jerec located the Valley of the Jedi on the planet [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]], and his forces occupied the planet. However, Jerec was tracked down by an aspiring Jedi, [[Kyle Katarn]], who tried to stop the Dark Jedi. Maw [[Mission to Ruusan|confronted]] Katarn on a landing pad near the cargo [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] ''[[Sulon Star]]'' and was defeated. Immobilized and defenseless, he [[Dun Möch/Legends|taunted]] Katarn with remarks of his [[Morgan Katarn|father's]] death, trying to goad him into the dark side. Katarn succumbed to his anger and murdered Maw, but was ultimately able to resist his dark thoughts and remained with the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light]]. ==Biography== ===Early life=== [[File:Maw_Insider31.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Maw before his bisection]] Maw was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Boltrunian/Legends|Boltrunian]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] born more than seven decades before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. He served the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] during its waning [[Time/Legends|years]]. A selfish individual, Maw refused to take a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], instead focusing on rooting out any sign of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] as a member of the [[Jedi Shadow]]s, the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order's]] [[Jedi investigator/Legends|investigator]] corps. After the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] and the [[Declaration of a New Order|formation]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Maw became one of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] fugitives on the run from the Empire's agents, and during the early stages of the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]], he was listed as being at large by the [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]] as "Maw the Boltrunian."{{InsiderCite|84|Republic HoloNet News Special Inaugural Edition 16:5:241}} Eventually, however, [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] tracked Maw's group of Shadows. Taking advantage of the Boltrunian's self-oriented nature, Jerec succeeded in turning Maw to the dark side as well, and they [[Death/Legends|slaughtered]] the rest of the Shadows together. Taught by Jerec to [[Force sense/Legends|smell the fear]] of his prey, Maw became a servant of the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]],{{SW|url=news/barely-tolerable-alien-henchmen-of-the-empire-part-2|series=Barely Tolerable: Alien Henchmen of the Empire|text=Part 2|nolive=1}} and one of the earliest members of Jerec's cadre of [[seven Dark Jedi]]. During his service to the Emperor, Maw traveled to [[Dromund Kaas/Legends|Dromund Kaas]], a forgotten world that had once been a looming [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] colony. There, Maw discovered a group of hungry and destitute [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]] led by a warrior named [[KkH'Oar'Rrhr]]. The group was worshiping a former [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]–turned–Tusken tribal leader named [[Darth Krayt|A'Sharad Hett]], who had been shunned by the majority of Tusken Raiders on their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] for being [[Duel on Tatooine (Imperial Period)|defeated in battle]] and having his mask removed from his face in a disgrace of Tusken customs. After Hett left Tatooine, KkH'Oar'Rrhr and his followers tried to track him across the galaxy, but failed in doing so and ended up on Dromund Kaas. Maw reorganized the group into a [[Swoop gang/Legends|swoop gang]] loyal to the Empire and transported them to the ancient Jedi homeworld of [[Tython/Legends|Tython]], where they were tasked with robbing graves of valuable Force artifacts. The Boltrunian christened his newly formed squad of enforcers—who honored Hett by exposing their flesh—the "[[Grave Tuskens]]." In [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' provides the [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]] date for the events of ''[[Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire]]'', featuring the [[subjugation of Sulon]]. Jerec led an [[Subjugation of Sulon|assault]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Sulon/Legends|Sulon]] to capture [[Morgan Katarn]], a member of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. After failing to extract information regarding other Rebels from the captured Katarn, Jerec [[Sai cha/Legends|decapitated]] him,''[[Dark Forces: Soldier for the Empire]]'' and Maw with [[Boc Aseca]] adorned the [[City/Legends|city]] city wall of [[Barons Hed]] with the heads of the killed Rebels for display. Maw personally thrust Morgan's head on a spike. ===Search for the Valley=== {{Quote|Have you captured them yet?''"
"''No, my Lord, but Maw has sensed their presence.''"
"''Excellent, excellent. You must trust Maw's Force-sensing ability, Sariss. Maw's power lacks the refinement that you and your young companion Yun possess, but they are a force to be reckoned with.|Jerec and Sariss, during the search for Qu Rahn|[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' audio drama]]}} With Palpatine's death in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Maw continued his service to Jerec as one of his seven Dark Jedi, alongside [[Sariss]], [[Boc Aseca]], [[Gorc]], [[Picaroon C. Boodle]], and [[Yun]]. Maw and the other [[darksider]]s joined the Inquisitor's search for the [[Valley of the Jedi]], a [[Vergence/Legends|Force nexus]] containing numerous [[Force ghost/Legends|Jedi and Sith souls]] and the source of great power, which Jerec sought to harness.[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II#Game manual|''Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II'' Game manual]]''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'' In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]],''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'', which features the [[Mission to Dorlo|capture]] of [[Qu Rahn/Legends|Qu Rahn]] to [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]]. Jerec discovered that a Jedi Master named [[Qu Rahn/Legends|Qu Rahn]] had some clues to the location of the Valley and tracked him to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dorlo]], where Rahn was on a [[Mission to Dorlo|mission]] on behalf of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. [[Blockade/Legends|Blockading]] the planet with his [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]], the [[Vengeance (Vengeance-class)|''Vengeance'']], Jerec dispatched Maw, Sariss, and Yun to the surface to capture Rahn. The assault team consisted of two [[Skimmer/Legends|skimmers]], one carrying the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedis]] and a [[Pilot/Legends|helmsman]], and the other some Imperial [[Troop/Legends|troops]]. Using his ability to sense the fear of his prey, Maw navigated the skimmers, following Rahn's trail. The skimmer with the troops was soon destroyed by one of the members from Rahn's group, buying the rest of Rahn's team time to reach [[Duno Dree's transport|their]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]]. [[File:AwkwardDarkJediParty-JK.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Maw forcing Rahn to his knees aboard the ''Vengeance'']] As Rahn's team departed the planet, [[Duno Dree]] attempted to attack the dark-siders's skimmer; Maw spotted it first, roared and armed his lightsaber to defend himself, but the vessel was caught in the [[Corvette/Legends|corvette's]] slipstream. The helmsman could not handle the controls and rammed the skimmer into a stonewall. Maw jumped in time, and everyone aboard survived the impact, but, angered because of the mission's failure, Maw beheaded the helmsman. Rahn, however, was not able to leave the planet, as the corvette was caught in the ''Vengeance''{{'s}} [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]]. Rahn and his companions were escorted to the ship's [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]], where Jerec, accompanied by his Dark Jedi, and after expressing his disappointment to the trio who failed to capture them on the planet, he started interrogating Rahn. The Jedi Master refused to answer, even after the rest of his team were executed one by one, and was released by the guards, only to be [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] by Maw. After the floor was stained with the old Jedi's blood by Maw's blows, he expected for one more kick from the big creature, when Jerec stopped him; he approached the Jedi and with a touch [[Drain Knowledge|ripped information]] from Rahn's mind. Maw then bore witness as the Jedi Master [[Telekinesis/Legends|Force-pulled]] [[Yun's lightsaber]] from his belt and attacked the seven Dark Jedi. Boc and then Sariss faield to stop him, and Maw lunged at Rahn, but the Jedi Master performed a ''[[Sai tok/Legends|Sai tok]]'' maneuver and severed Maw's [[Limb/Legends|legs]] from his torso; by that time [[Medic/Legends|medics]] had arrived to the bridge and pulled him clear, leaving his legs behind. Moments later, Rahn was killed by Jerec. Drawing from his hate, Maw survived his injury. He replaced his lower body with a [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] carriage to preserve mobility, but he soon learned to rely only on the dark side by mastering the art of [[The Force/Legends|Force]] flight. Flying became Maw's second nature, enabling him to become one of the few persons who appropriated the [[Trispzest]] style of [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]] initially developed by the airborne [[S'kytri]] [[Magister of Skye|Majestrix]] [[Kharys]]; in this method, he used a devastating spinning lightsaber attack. From that time on, Maw also became known as "the Half-Man."''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'' In the meantime, having read Rahn's thoughts, Jerec learned that the man he had executed years ago, Morgan Katarn, had known the coordinates of the Valley and had hidden the information leading to it somewhere on his [[Farm/Legends|farm]] on Sulon. Eventually, Jerec located the Valley on the planet [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]] and moved his forces there, establishing an excavation operation on the planet's surface. ===Final duel=== [[File:Maw_kyle.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Maw in a fierce duel with Kyle Katarn]] {{Quote|You're weak like your father. I remember it. Jerec, he gave him a sweet slow death—a death worthy of a coward! I had the honor of taking his head and thrusting it on a spike for all to see.|Maw taunts Kyle Katarn|audio=MawTauntsKyle-JKDF2.flac|}} Soon, however, the Jedi [[Kyle Katarn]]—the son of Morgan Katarn—[[Mission to Ruusan|infiltrated]] Ruusan in an effort to stop Jerec. Having sensed his presence in the Force, Maw awaited Katarn at the docking platform of the [[Valley Tower]] occupied by Jerec's personal cargo [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]], the ''[[Sulon Star]]''. As soon as Katarn emerged from the [[Turbolift/Legends|turbolift]] that took him to the platform, Maw attacked him from behind, knocking him onto the walkway, goading him into a [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|duel]]. Katarn, however, taunted Maw with remarks about how Rahn had cut him in half; enraged, Maw charged at Katarn with great speed, but the Jedi evaded the attack and injured Maw severely with a counterattack. Maw fell onto the walkway and taunted the man with lies about his father's cowardly death, trying to force Katarn to kill an unarmed opponent and give in to the dark side. He partially succeeded in this, as Katarn acquiesced to his anger and killed the injured, immobilized, and unarmed Maw, but cutting diagonally his remaining torso in half. Maw's last feeling was the delicious smell of Katarn's loathing, as he came closer to the dark side. However, when Jerec arrived immediately afterward, offering Katarn a chance to join his cadre by executing captured [[Jan Ors]], Katarn's partner, the young Jedi refused to do so and stayed true to the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side]]. Ultimately, Katarn was able to defeat the remaining Dark Jedi and killed Jerec, preventing him from possessing the power of the Valley. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Finally I get to wipe your ''putrid'' taste from the Force!|Maw, to Kyle Katarn|}} Maw was a very selfish, animalistic, and brutish individual—traits that, combined with his unwillingness to take a Padawan, contributed to the fact that he never became a Jedi Master. During his days as a Jedi, he was determined to root out any sign of the dark side. Already a relentless tracker, his skills were improved by Jerec, and he took pleasure in sensing the fear of his prey, leading Yun to compare him to a [[Nek/Legends|Nek battle dog]]. Even in the moments before his death, Maw enjoyed the strong feeling of loathing that Katarn emanated. Maw fed on anger and hatred to the point that they allowed him to survive his [[Dismemberment/Legends|dismemberment]]. He took the loss of his legs in stride, quickly adapting to use the Force to fly. Maw was also short-tempered and could not stand Katarn taunting him, responding by attacking him in anger. The Boltrunian had [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. Maw had diligently invested much in intense grav-workouts to hone his body; he used his anger, letting the Dark Side to flow with his bodily fluids. In his later life Maw was a massive volume of sturdy muscle. He managed to feed off the hate of others so that the Dark Side didn't corrupt his body, and as a result, he looked several decades younger than his actual age. ==Powers and abilities== {{Quote|I sense fear, that is my talent. I taste it, I smell it, fear sends ripples through the dark side of the Force and brings the sweet feeling to me.|Maw|}} [[File:MawLevitates-DarkForces2.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Maw using the dark side of the Force to levitate.]] Maw's most well-developed ability was the variation of Force sense that allowed him to smell the fear and panic of his foes like a trained [[Blood/Legends|bloodsniffer]]. Jerec himself held this ability in great esteem, although he believed that it lacked subtlety. Maw used his skill on Dorlo to follow the trail of Rahn. When he witnessed Rahn's corvette blasting off the planet, his senses told him that his enemies' fear was great and that they would try to escape. On Ruusan, he also sensed Kyle Katarn in the Force, considering his taste to be "putrid." The other power that Maw mastered was [[Telekinesis/Legends#Force Flight|Force flight]], which, initially employed to preserve his mobility, soon became his second nature; the Boltrunian found many advantages in it and performed it easily. When he utilized Force flight, his usage of the Force extended to small objects in the vicinity, and various bolts, pebbles, and wires orbited his body like planets [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] a [[Star/Legends|star]]. To balance his flying skills, he also learned the art of Trispzest, the flying combat that combined the ferocity of the seventh lightsaber form [[Form VII/Legends|Juyo]] with traditional S'kytri flying combat techniques, and made use of an aerial "Whirlwind Attack". Although Maw's talent [[Jedi Guardian/Legends|laid in battle]], his fighting skills were deficient, as he was easily defeated by Rahn and performed a sluggish attack that was easily evaded and counteracted by the inexperienced Katarn. He had some skill in [[Force healing/Legends|Force healing]], [[Tutaminis|Drain Energy]], and telekinesis. In addition to these powers, Maw was proficient in the use of some simple weapons and [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistols]]. He could write and speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]]. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Maw_DFJK.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Maw, seen here traveling with Sariss and Yun on Dorlo, is mistakenly depicted without legs even though this takes place before the encounter with Qu Rahn.]] The character of Maw first appeared in the [[1997]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'', in which he served as one of the antagonists. As the game featured full-motion video cutscenes, Maw was portrayed by actor [[Morgan Hunter]]. The character also appeared in the two novelizations of the game, ''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'' and ''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'', written by [[William C. Dietz]]. Additionally, Maw was featured in the audio dramatizations of both [[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Rebel Agent'']] and [[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight (audio drama)|''Jedi Knight'']],[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Jedi Knight'' audio drama]] in which he was voiced by [[Martin Ruben]].''[[Dark Forces: The Collector's Trilogy]]'' The article [[Building a Better Jedi: Justin Chin on Dark Forces II|''Building a Better Jedi'']] from [[Star Wars Insider 31|''Star Wars Insider'' 31]] was the first source to delve into Maw's background. According to it, Maw was a tortured soul who grew in the streets and started his speeder gang, before becoming a Dark Jedi and eventually joining Jerec.{{InsiderCite|31|Building a Better Jedi: Justin Chin on Dark Forces II|Building a Better Jedi: Justin Chin on ''Dark Forces II''}} Maw's backstory was rewritten in the [http://www.wizards.com/default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050526a fourth installment] of ''[[The Dark Forces Saga]]'', a series of web scenarios written by [[Abel G. Peña]] and [[Jason Fry]] and published on [[Wizards.com]], which contained completely different and contradictory information regarding Maw's early life such as that he was a former Jedi. Maw is mistakenly depicted without legs in ''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' in scenes that occur before his clash with Rahn aboard the ''Vengeance''. Additionally, in the audio dramatization of ''Rebel Agent'', it is Sariss who executes the skimmer's pilot instead of Maw. The audio dramatization also omits the scene of Rahn attacking the Dark Jedi aboard the ''Vengeance''. Instead, Rahn accepts his fate and is killed immediately after being robbed of his memories. This way, Maw never loses his legs in the audio drama. In a [[Wikipedia:Twitter|Twitter]] [http://twitter.com/#!/HolocronKeeper/status/124349361630093312 post] left by [[Lucasfilm]] [[Holocron continuity database]] keeper [[Leland Chee]] on [[October 13]] of [[2011]], Chee provided Maw as an example of an individual who survived being cut in half. This way, he hinted at the potential reappearance of the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] in the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' television series, after the latter was sliced in half by Jedi [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] in a similar manner to Maw in the [[1999]] film [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']] and was presumed dead since then.{{Twitter|HolocronKeeper|status/124349361630093312|[[Leland Chee]]|quote=Remember Maw from Dark Force II Jedi Knight? He survived getting cut in half also.|archivefile=File:Chee Maw tweet.png}} ==Appearances== *{{InsiderCite|84|Republic HoloNet News Special Inaugural Edition 16:5:241}} *''[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent]]'' *[[Dark Forces: Rebel Agent (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Rebel Agent'' audio drama]] *''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'' {{1st}} *''[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight]]'' *[[Dark Forces: Jedi Knight (audio drama)|''Dark Forces: Jedi Knight'' audio drama]] ==Sources== {{ScrollBox|content= {{Mediacat|soundcat=Audio files of Maw (Dark Jedi)|imagecat=Images of Maw}} *{{InsiderCite|31|Building a Better Jedi: Justin Chin on Dark Forces II|Building a Better Jedi: Justin Chin on ''Dark Forces II''}} *{{GalaxyCite|13|Game Room|Game Room: Game Knights}} {{Po}} *''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' *{{LucasArts|url=static/jk/html/characters.html|text=Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II - Characters}} *{{InsiderCite|40|The Horse's Mouth|Straight from the Horse's Mouth}} *[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] {{Imo}} *''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]'' {{Imo}} *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050407a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 1}} {{Imo}} *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050407b|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 2: Unsung Heroes of the Light}} {{Imo}} *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050512b|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 3: Pride of the Dark Side}} {{Imo}} *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050526a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 4: Of the Crudest Matter}} *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050609a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 5: Two Peas in a Pod}} {{Imo}} *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050616a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 6: Outcasts and Megalomaniacs Welcome}} {{Imo}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[Rogue Leaders: The Story of LucasArts]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{StarshipsVehiclesCite|27|Star Wars A–Z}} {{Imo}} *{{DB|character|kylekatarn|Katarn, Kyle}} *{{DB|character|sariss|Sariss}} {{Imo}} *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' *{{SW|url=news/barely-tolerable-alien-henchmen-of-the-empire-part-2|series=Barely Tolerable: Alien Henchmen of the Empire|text=Part 2|nolive=1}} }} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Maw |es=Maw (Jedi Oscuro) |it=Maw (Jedi) |pl=Maw |pt=Maw (Jedi Negro) }} [[Category:Boltrunians]] [[Category:Cyborgs with prosthetics]] [[Category:Fallen Jedi]] [[Category:Individuals with lost limbs]] [[Category:Jedi Guardians]] [[Category:Jedi Purge survivors]] [[Category:Jedi Shadows]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Post-Ruusan Jedi Knights]] [[Category:Seven Dark Jedi]]
